skill.name: accessibility-compliance-accessibility-audit
skill.description: Audits digital interfaces against WCAG accessibility requirements and provides practical remediation guidance for inclusive user experiences.
skill.summary: Run WCAG-focused accessibility audits with automated checks, manual testing guidance, and remediation steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but the Ruby backtick, weak crypto, credential key, reconnaissance, and critical heuristic alerts are false positives from markdown fences, WCAG labels, keyboard examples, and local CI snippets. No prompt injection, credential theft, or malicious exfiltration evidence was found. The remaining risk is medium because sample code shows arbitrary URL browser navigation and unescaped HTML report generation that need guardrails if copied into production.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary URL Browser Navigation in Sample Auditor
medium_findings.description: The playbook sample accepts a URL and sends Puppeteer to that address. This is expected for accessibility auditing, but a hosted service using this code should restrict targets to prevent internal network access or unintended browsing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The browser navigation to a caller-provided URL is explicit. The risk depends on deployment context, so confidence is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Unescaped HTML Report Interpolation in Sample Code
medium_findings.description: The report generator builds HTML with audit result fields inserted directly into the template. If future data includes user-controlled text, copied production code could create cross-site scripting risk unless values are escaped.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The HTML interpolation is visible in the sample. Exploitability depends on whether the audit data is trusted, so the finding is a moderate implementation risk.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Commands in CI Example Require Normal Supply Chain Controls
low_findings.description: The CI example runs npm and npx commands for accessibility testing. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but projects copying it should pin and review dependencies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and related to accessibility testing. The concern is standard supply chain hygiene, not evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTML Template Construction Without Escaping
dangerous_patterns.description: The sample report template inserts audit values into HTML without showing escaping. Treat this as a dangerous pattern if report data can include untrusted content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly constructs HTML with interpolated values. The severity is bounded because it is documentation sample code.

content.value_statement: Accessibility defects block users and create compliance risk. This skill guides WCAG audits with automated checks, manual verification, and clear remediation steps.
actual_capabilities: Plans audits around WCAG levels, target pages, and critical user journeys.
Uses axe-core and jest-axe style automated checks as baseline examples.
Covers manual keyboard, screen reader, focus order, contrast, and reflow checks.
Maps findings to user impact, severity, and remediation actions.
Provides CI workflow examples for ongoing accessibility regression testing.
limitations: Automated tests cannot prove full WCAG compliance without manual review.
Screen reader findings depend on the tested assistive technology and platform.
The skill needs access to pages, components, designs, or audit evidence.
Legal compliance decisions require qualified legal or accessibility governance review.

use_cases.title: Audit a Product Flow Before Release
use_cases.description: Review checkout, onboarding, or account flows for barriers and prioritize fixes before launch.

use_cases.title: Add Accessibility Tests to Development
use_cases.description: Create automated checks and manual review steps that fit pull requests and CI pipelines.

use_cases.title: Prepare Compliance Evidence
use_cases.description: Organize issues, WCAG mappings, retest results, and remediation notes for stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility issues. Focus on keyboard access, labels, headings, contrast, and clear remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Component Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a UI component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component for accessibility barriers. Include semantic HTML, ARIA use, focus behavior, error states, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Compliance Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit across a user journey
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this user journey against WCAG 2.2 AA. Group findings by step, severity, user impact, acceptance criteria, and retest method.

prompt_templates.title: Program Improvement Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an accessibility testing program for this product team. Include automated gates, manual checks, ownership, reporting, and release criteria.

output_examples.input: Audit a login form for accessibility risks.
output: Findings grouped by keyboard access, labels, errors, contrast, and focus visibility.
Each issue includes WCAG mapping, severity, user impact, and a remediation recommendation.
A retest checklist confirms the corrected form works with keyboard and screen reader use.

output_examples.input: Create a CI accessibility testing approach for a React app.
output: Recommended automated checks for component tests and browser-level scans.
Manual review tasks for screen reader behavior and complex interactions.
Guidance on thresholds, artifacts, and when to block a release.

output_examples.input: Prioritize accessibility issues from an audit report.
output: A ranked remediation plan based on severity, frequency, and user journey impact.
Clear ownership suggestions for design, engineering, content, and QA.
Acceptance criteria that teams can use during implementation and retesting.

best_practices: Define the WCAG version, conformance level, pages, and assistive technologies before testing.
Combine automated scans with manual keyboard, screen reader, contrast, and reflow checks.
Retest fixes and keep evidence with dates, tools, pages, and remaining exceptions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat automated scan results as complete proof of accessibility compliance.
Do not add ARIA before checking whether native HTML already provides the needed semantics.
Do not lower severity only because an issue affects a smaller user group.

faq.question: Does this skill certify WCAG compliance?
faq.answer: No. It supports audit work and remediation planning, but certification requires formal governance and qualified review.

faq.question: Which WCAG level does it support?
faq.answer: It can guide WCAG A and AA audits, with examples focused on common WCAG 2.x AA checks.

faq.question: Can it replace screen reader testing?
faq.answer: No. It can plan and interpret screen reader tests, but real assistive technology testing is still required.

faq.question: Can it work with design files?
faq.answer: Yes, if the relevant screens, components, colors, text, and interaction details are available.

faq.question: What evidence should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide target URLs, screenshots, component code, user journeys, existing scan output, and the required WCAG level.

faq.question: Is this only for web applications?
faq.answer: The examples focus on web testing, but the audit structure can support mobile and product accessibility reviews.
